How it works

From listing to max bid
in seconds

BidMax runs quietly in the background while you browse. No extra clicks, no separate tools — just numbers on every card.

1

Open BidRL normally

Browse any BidRL auction page on desktop or mobile. BidMax detects all the lots automatically and queues them for analysis the moment the page loads.

2

AI prices each lot

Every lot title is analyzed against real, local Facebook Marketplace prices — not retail, not eBay. Conservative estimates to protect your margin.

3

Bid with confidence

Max bid and estimated profit appear on each card. 🔥 flags mark your best opportunities so you can spot them instantly while scrolling.

The math

A formula that
protects your margin.

Set your numbers once. BidMax does the rest — every lot, every time.

Example — Samsung Washer Dryer Combo
FB Resale Value (estimated)$1,650
FB Marketplace Fee (5%)−$83
Your Target Profit (30%)−$495
Effort Cost (pickup, time)−$10
BidRL Buyer's Premium (15%)÷ 1.15
Your Max Bid$923
FAQ

Common questions

BidRL is an online auction platform that partners with liquidation warehouses across the United States. Buyers can bid on lots of returned, overstock, or used merchandise — including furniture, appliances, electronics, and more — and pick up winning items from a local warehouse location.

A buyer's premium is a percentage fee added on top of your winning bid price. For example, if you win a lot at $100 and the buyer's premium is 18%, your total cost is $118. BidMax automatically factors your buyer's premium into the max bid calculation so the number you see is what you actually pay at the door.

BidRL runs timed auctions on individual lots. You place a bid before the auction closes — if you're the highest bidder when time expires, you win. You then pay your winning bid plus the buyer's premium, and pick up the item at the affiliated warehouse. Items are sold as-is, so knowing the resale value before you bid is critical.

BidMax estimates what each lot will sell for on Facebook Marketplace, then works backwards through your costs: Facebook's selling fee, your buyer's premium, your effort cost, and your target profit margin. The result is the highest bid you can place and still hit your profit goal. You set your numbers once in settings and BidMax applies them to every lot automatically.

Facebook Marketplace resale value is what a real buyer in your local area would actually pay for the item today. BidMax uses AI to estimate this based on the lot title and description — not retail price, not eBay. Local Facebook Marketplace prices are typically 30–60% below retail, which is why using real resale value protects your margin far better than guessing.

Yes — BidMax has a free tier across all platforms: the Chrome extension, Android app, and iOS app. Each gives you a set number of analyses per day to get started. The Pro plan ($9.99/month) unlocks unlimited analyses across all three. No credit card required to start.

BidMax is available as a Chrome browser extension, an Android app, and an iOS app. The Chrome extension overlays max bid and profit data directly onto BidRL.com as you browse. The mobile apps let you analyze lots from your phone — useful when you're walking an auction floor or checking deals on the go.

The basic model is: buy low at auction, sell locally for a profit. The key is knowing what something is worth on Facebook Marketplace before you bid — not after. Most resellers lose money by overbidding on items they can't sell for what they expected. BidMax solves this by giving you the resale estimate and max bid before you place a single dollar.

The best categories for BidRL flipping on Facebook Marketplace are appliances (washers, dryers, refrigerators), furniture (sofas, beds, dressers), and power tools. These items have high resale value relative to auction prices and consistent local demand. Electronics can be profitable but carry more risk due to condition uncertainty.
